title: linum rigidum var. rigidum
intro: Linum rigidum var. rigidum, also known as Stiff Flax, is a perennial herb in the flax family, characterized by its upright stems and yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, making it beneficial for various health issues, including skin irritations and digestive problems. Stiff Flax has been traditionally used to treat conditions such as eczema, wounds, and respiratory issues, and has also been used as a natural laxative and to reduce fever. Herbal preparations of Stiff Flax include teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
